skill.name: spatial-transcriptomics-mapper
skill.description: Map spatial transcriptomics data from 10x Genomics Visium and Xenium platforms onto tissue section images for gene expression visualization and spatial clustering analysis.
skill.summary: A bioinformatics tool that projects gene expression data back onto tissue images, enabling visual analysis of spatial transcriptomics datasets.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 121 static analysis findings are false positives or represent legitimate intended functionality. The flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference argparse description strings, license declarations, and documentation table headers. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are all markdown code fence blocks in the SKILL.md documentation file. Path traversal and system reconnaissance findings reference security checklist items, error messages, and HTML templates. No actual malicious code, data exfiltration, or unauthorized network access was found.

low_findings.title: Python file write for report generation
low_findings.description: File write at main.py:789 is legitimate output generation (writing HTML report). The tool writes analysis results to user-specified output directory. No arbitrary or unauthorized file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File write is the intended output functionality for generating HTML reports and PNG images from spatial transcriptomics analysis.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs in SKILL.md
low_findings.description: URLs at SKILL.md:7, 314, 319-322 are documentation references (GitHub repo, matplotlib docs, 10x Genomics, Scanpy, Squidpy). These are citations, not network requests from executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are standard documentation and library references with no executable network calls in the actual Python code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Ruby/shell backtick false positives from markdown formatting (94 instances)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged all markdown code fence blocks and inline backtick formatting in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. SKILL.md is a documentation file using standard markdown ```bash, ```text, ```python, and `inline_code` formatting. No actual shell execution is occurring.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The SKILL.md file is purely documentation. All backtick usage is standard markdown syntax for code blocks and inline code formatting. The Python scripts use subprocess, not backticks.

content.user_title: Map gene expression onto tissue images with spatial transcriptomics
content.value_statement: Researchers need to visualize where specific genes are expressed within tissue sections, but raw spatial transcriptomics data is hard to interpret. This skill processes 10x Genomics Visium and Xenium data to project gene expression onto tissue images, producing publication-ready maps and clustering visualizations.
actual_capabilities: Process Visium Space Ranger output files (h5, CSV, PNG) and visualize gene expression on tissue images
Process Xenium output files (h5, parquet) and visualize transcript-level gene expression
Generate multi-gene overlay maps showing spatial co-expression patterns across tissue sections
Read and plot Seurat or Scanpy clustering results on spatial coordinates
Export high-resolution PNG images at configurable DPI settings for publication use
Generate comprehensive HTML reports combining gene expression maps, statistics tables, and cluster information
limitations: Requires pre-processed 10x Genomics pipeline output (Space Ranger or Xenium Explorer) as input
Does not perform cell type annotation or differential expression analysis
Large Xenium datasets may need manual region-of-interest cropping for performance
No interactive 3D visualization or web-based viewer for spatial data exploration

use_cases.title: Visualize tumor marker distribution in cancer tissue
use_cases.description: A cancer researcher has Visium data from a breast tumor biopsy and wants to see where PIK3CA, ERBB2, and ESR1 are expressed across the tissue section. This skill generates an overlay map showing co-expression patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Cancer researcher with Visium spatial gene expression data

use_cases.title: Validate spatial clustering annotations
use_cases.description: A bioinformatician has computed Seurat clustering on Visium data and needs to verify that cluster boundaries align with tissue morphology visible in the H&E image. This skill overlays cluster assignments on the tissue image.

use_cases.title: Explore subcellular gene localization in Xenium data
use_cases.description: A lung biology researcher has Xenium in situ data for lung tissue and wants to examine SFTPB and SFTPC transcript distribution at subcellular resolution across different tissue regions.
use_cases.target_user: Researcher analyzing Xenium subcellular resolution gene expression data

prompt_templates.title: Single gene expression mapping from Visium data
prompt_templates.prompt: I have 10x Visium spatial transcriptomics data from a breast cancer biopsy at ./data/visium_sample/outs/. Help me visualize where the EPCAM gene is expressed on the tissue section. Use a red color scheme and save the output to ./results/.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher wants to examine expression of a single gene of interest on a standard Visium tissue section.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-gene co-expression overlay on tissue image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the spatial-transcriptomics-mapper to process Visium data from ./breast_cancer/outs/. I need to see the spatial distribution of four tumor marker genes: PIK3CA, ERBB2, ESR1, and PGR. Generate a multi-gene overlay map using the plasma color scheme and save to ./tumor_markers/.
prompt_templates.scenario: A cancer researcher wants to examine co-expression patterns of multiple biomarker genes simultaneously.

prompt_templates.title: Xenium subcellular transcript visualization
prompt_templates.prompt: Load Xenium data from ./xenium_lung/outs/ using the spatial-transcriptomics-mapper. Plot the spatial distribution of SFTPB, SFTPC, and SCGB1A1 transcripts at high resolution (600 DPI) and save the output to ./xenium_results/.
prompt_templates.scenario: A lung biology researcher wants to visualize transcripts at subcellular resolution from Xenium in situ data.

prompt_templates.title: Spatial clustering validation from pre-computed clusters
prompt_templates.prompt: I have Visium data at ./sample/outs/ and a pre-computed Seurat clustering CSV file at ./seurat_clusters.csv. Use the spatial-transcriptomics-mapper to overlay these cluster assignments on the tissue image. Save the cluster spatial map to ./clusters/.
prompt_templates.scenario: A bioinformatician needs to validate that computational clustering results correspond to visible tissue morphology.

output_examples.input: Visium breast cancer dataset with single gene query (EPCAM)
output: epcam_spatial_map.png - Tissue section image with EPCAM expression overlaid as a color gradient from low (dark) to high (bright)
epcam_heatmap.png - Heatmap showing relative EPCAM expression levels across all Visium spots arranged by spatial position
combined_report.html - Interactive HTML report combining the spatial map, expression statistics, and sample metadata

output_examples.input: Visium dataset with multi-gene breast cancer markers (PIK3CA, ERBB2, ESR1, PGR)
output: multi_gene_overlay.png - Tissue section with each gene shown in a distinct color (red, green, blue, yellow), revealing co-expression regions as color combinations
multi_gene_grid.png - Grid layout showing each gene's expression pattern in a separate subplot for side-by-side comparison

best_practices: Always confirm that the input data directory contains the expected Space Ranger or Xenium Explorer output structure before running the analysis
Use the --crop parameter for large Xenium datasets to focus on a region of interest and improve processing speed
Set the --dpi parameter to 300 for standard figures or 600 for publication-quality output images
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for RNA-seq bulk data or single-cell RNA-seq data without spatial coordinates - it requires spatial position information
Do not skip input validation; verify that gene names in your query match the gene names present in the dataset
Do not attempt to process data from unsupported platforms - only Visium (Space Ranger) and Xenium (Xenium Explorer) formats are accepted

faq.question: What input data formats does this skill accept?
faq.answer: This skill accepts 10x Genomics Visium Space Ranger output (filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5, tissue_positions_list.csv, tissue_lowres_image.png) and Xenium Explorer output (.h5, transcripts.parquet, nucleus_boundaries.parquet).

faq.question: Does this skill require internet access?
faq.answer: No. The skill processes local data files and generates output images and reports locally. All documentation URLs in the skill description are reference links only and are not accessed during execution.

faq.question: What output files does this skill produce?
faq.answer: Output files include PNG images (gene spatial maps, heatmaps, cluster maps, multi-gene overlays) and an optional comprehensive HTML report combining all results with statistics.

faq.question: Can this skill process data from other spatial platforms?
faq.answer: No. This skill only supports 10x Genomics Visium (Space Ranger output) and Xenium (Xenium Explorer output) data formats. Other platforms such as MERFISH, Slide-seq, or Visium HD are not supported.

faq.question: Does this skill perform cell type annotation or differential expression analysis?
faq.answer: No. This skill focuses on mapping and visualizing gene expression on tissue images. It does not perform cell type identification, differential expression analysis, or statistical hypothesis testing.

faq.question: What dependencies are required to run this skill?
faq.answer: Required dependencies include Python 3.10+, scanpy, squidpy, matplotlib, seaborn, pillow, numpy, pandas, and h5py. For Xenium data, pyarrow is also needed. All are listed in requirements.txt.
